title: C++ shorthand authoring is the inverse of the read frontend
summary: Space-triggered dollar forms expand into real C++ while the presentation frontend renders many of those stored forms back into the compact visual language.
details: cpp_type_snippets.lua activates only in c, cpp, and cuda buffers and expands a complete dollar block when Space is pressed.

Type atoms and templates compose in prefix or parenthesized forms; missing operands become literal dollar placeholders so underspecified expansions remain visibly incomplete, while overspecified forms are rejected.
It covers standard containers, optionals, smart pointers, arrays, casts, concept relations, and copy/move special members.
A line-end preview shows partial resolution without modifying source; DansCppFormat is a separate authoring command.
Authoring expansion changes buffer bytes intentionally and is therefore outside the presentation-only invariant's transform category.

provenance.note: The module documents and implements grammar, resolution, preview, formatting, mappings, and commands.

evidence.claim: Composition, missing/extra arguments, strings, casts, special members, and formatting are pinned.

questions: How do I author the compact type syntax without storing it?
Why may snippets rewrite source when the frontend may not?
What happens when a shorthand is missing an operand?

last_verified.method: Superseded after interaction ownership moved from cpp_type_snippets.lua into the first-party authoring engine.
